title: Analyst Workstation — eight-page interactive AI-augmented intelligence-analysis handbook

fields.vision: An analyst workstation for AI-augmented intelligence analysis.
fields.mechanism: It converts AI support into inspection surfaces: knowledge production, prompt engineering discipline, cognitive-bias safeguards, Key Assumptions Check, live SAT visualization instruments, source review, ICD/NIST-bound assurance language, analyst-model disagreement, and human acceptance or correction before conclusions move forward. The cross-page workstation can connect AI Governance Framework Navigator, EU AI Act considerations, AI System Assessment, AI Failure Mode Taxonomy, OSINT Narrative Mapping, OSIRIS LLM, and per-stage AI/HITL/ICD/NIST checks.
fields.built_components: All nine pages render at their intended fidelity. Each instrument page declares its expert roster and its citation count, and in aggregate the eight instrument pages reference more than five hundred citations across the major intelligence-studies and AI-governance authorities — Heuer, George, Lowenthal, Kent, Fingar, Bullock, Pashentsev, Beebe and Pherson, alongside the ODNI, NIST, EU AI Act, and Stanford HAI corpora. The citation infrastructure is in place; what is not yet built is the source-register chain that would bind each declared citation to an admitted snippet from the underlying corpus. The citations sit at an early stage where the citation infrastructure is in place but not yet bound to admitted source snippets, and the portfolio's governance overlay refuses stronger language than "declared" until that binding is built. The parent Analyst Assurance Desk renders the suite's coherent shell, but the cross-page evidence aggregator that would let an analyst see a SAT instrument in Page 7 trace back to its supporting Heuer passage in Page 1 is the next major build — it is the move that converts the suite from eight related instruments into a connected reasoning surface.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Graduate the suite from declared citations to provable citations — wire the source-register chain that the pages already announce as missing. The order is dictated by leverage: the densest pages get the binding work first, because that is where the discipline is most visible to a reader. Step 01 High-density pages first AI-Augmented Intelligence Cycle Synthesis and Knowledge Production — the densest pages — lifted first, where the source-register pilot has the most leverage. Step 02 Cross-page evidence aggregator Built so an analyst can follow a claim across pages — a SAT instrument in one page traces back to the Heuer passage that grounds it in another. Step 03 Six-page propagation Source-register binding propagated to the remaining six instrument pages so the suite becomes uniformly fully citation-bound. Step 04 Citation-roster verification Heuer / George / Lowenthal / Kent / Fingar / Bullock / Pashentsev / Beebe-Pherson / ODNI / NIST / EU AI Act / Stanford HAI all verified against the admitted-snippet store; declared roster becomes provable roster.
fields.status: Status: All nine pages render at their intended fidelity. Each instrument page declares its expert roster and its citation count, and in aggregate the eight instrument pages reference more than five hundred citations across the major intelligence-studies and AI-governance authorities — Heuer, George, Lowenthal, Kent, Fingar, Bullock, Pashentsev, Beebe and Pherson, alongside the ODNI, NIST, EU AI Act, and Stanford HAI corpora.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: graduate the suite from declared citations to provable citations — wire the source-register chain that the pages already announce as missing. The order is dictated by leverage: the densest pages get the binding work first, because that is where the discipline is most visible to a reader.
fields.detailed_source_vision: An interactive handbook organized as a parent Analyst Assurance Desk hub plus eight instrument pages, where each page is the actual screen an analyst would work in rather than an explanation of what such a screen might look like. The Bible-building methodology in the knowledge-production page is the methodology an analyst would use; the prompt-engineering page is an explicit mapping from Heuer's biases to specific prompt safeguards; the SAT visualization page carries genuinely interactive ACH and Key Assumptions Check instruments operating on an analyst's input. The handbook is governed throughout by the same Classified Workstation design system the rest of the project's portfolio uses, on the explicit aesthetic claim that this is the bureaucratic materiality of intelligence work, not its dramatized version.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The parent Analyst Assurance Desk gives the suite its coherent professional shell; the eight spokes are the actual instrument pages an analyst would work in. Each page carries its own expert roster and its own citation density, and the variation in citation density across pages is itself a diagnostic — the ICD/NIST-bound governance pages run dense, the live SAT instruments run thinner because they share primitives with the rest of the portfolio.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: An analyst workstation for AI-augmented intelligence analysis.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It converts AI support into inspection surfaces: knowledge production, prompt engineering discipline, cognitive-bias safeguards, Key Assumptions Check, live SAT visualization instruments, source review, ICD/NIST-bound assurance language, analyst-model disagreement, and human acceptance or correction before conclusions move forward. The cross-page workstation can connect AI Governance Framework Navigator, EU AI Act considerations, AI System Assessment, AI Failure Mode Taxonomy, OSINT Narrative Mapping, OSIRIS LLM, and per-stage AI/HITL/ICD/NIST checks.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because human-in-the-loop review only has value when the human has a concrete workstation for inspecting sources, uncertainty, assumptions, structured analytic technique outputs, and reasoning paths rather than a generic prompt window.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: An analyst workstation for AI-augmented intelligence analysis.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because human-in-the-loop review only has value when the human has a concrete workstation for inspecting sources, uncertainty, assumptions, structured analytic technique outputs, and reasoning paths rather than a generic prompt window.

instruments.input: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.operation: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
instruments.output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: All nine pages render at their intended fidelity. Each instrument page declares its expert roster and its citation count, and in aggregate the eight.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role and produce method assignment and technique rationale as a.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through Task And Method Assignment; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.input: source record, assumptions, alternatives, indicators, confidence basis, and possible deception conditions
instruments.operation: separates evidence from assumptions before the analytic method begins; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
instruments.output: evidence/assumption ledger as a reviewable artifact

instruments.input: hypotheses, evidence rows, diagnosticity, contradiction, and consistency judgments

instruments.input: favored conclusion, assumptions, missing evidence, rival frame, and adversary incentives

instruments.output: challenge memo and revision demand as a reviewable artifact

instruments.input: AI output, human analyst judgment, source trail, uncertainty basis, and override reason

instruments.input: multiple SATs, task type, stage of inquiry, and analyst burden

instruments.output: release decision and method-learning record as a reviewable artifact
